title: Smoky Japanese Blackeye Pea Stir-fry
description: A low-fat-friendly salty and fluffy dish featuring blackeye peas with a hint of smokiness that can serve as a hangover cure.
instructions: Soak blackeye peas in water overnight.
Drain the soaking liquid from the peas. Rinse well and set aside.
In a large wok or skillet, heat sesame oil over medium-high heat.
Add minced garlic and ginger to the pan. Sauté for about 30 seconds until fragrant.
Stir in blackeye peas with soy sauce and rice vinegar. Mix well and cook for about 2 minutes to allow flavors to meld.
Fold in scallions, ensuring they are fully coated with the marinade mixture.
Toss sesame seeds into the stir-fry mixture and mix evenly for another minute or two until heated through.
Remove from heat immediately after tossing in the sesame seeds to avoid burning.
